headline: The Hiroshima Radiation Just Committed a Fantasy War Crime
subheadline: A 10-0 shutout, a tie that satisfied nobody, and the Wonderdogs continue their quest for world domination in Week 18.
body:
Look, I've seen some things in this league. I've watched grown adults name their team the **Buttville Skidmarks** and somehow maintain eye contact with the rest of us. But what **Hiroshima Radiation** did to **BaseballPro77** this week? That's the fantasy baseball equivalent of the Red Wedding. A 10-0 shutout. Ten to ZERO. BaseballPro77 didn't just lose—they got Thanos-snapped out of existence.

(Quick aside: BaseballPro77 is the most generic team name in the league, which makes this even funnier. It's like watching someone named 'John Smith' get dunked on at the YMCA by a guy in a full Spider-Man costume.)

The **Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs** continue to be the Golden State Warriors of this league—dominant, slightly annoying about it, and definitely the team everyone else is secretly hoping trips over their own shoelaces. They demolished the **Canberra Wombats** 8-1, which, honestly? The Wombats had it coming. You can't just waltz in here with your Australian marsupial energy and expect to compete at this level. This isn't the outback, friend. This is MillerLite® BeerLeagueBaseball.

Meanwhile, the **Albuquerque Bombers** knocked off the first-place **Sugar Land Skeeters** 7-3 in what should have been the marquee matchup of the week. The Skeeters have been flying high (get it? because they're... never mind), but the Bombers came in with that Breaking Bad energy—methodical, ruthless, probably wearing a hat.

Can we talk about the **Benchwarmers** vs **North Correa** ending in a 5-5 tie? A TIE. In 2024. This isn't soccer, people. Someone has to WIN. This is the "we need to talk" of fantasy results—nobody's happy, everyone's confused, and you're both going to bed angry.

(The standings are showing everyone at 0-0 with 0 points, which either means the commissioner is asleep at the wheel or we've entered some kind of fantasy baseball multiverse where nothing matters. I choose to believe it's the latter because that's more fun.)

The **Wisconsin Rapids Reapers** handled the **Buttville Skidmarks** 7-2, which—and I cannot stress this enough—is exactly what should happen when you name your team the Buttville Skidmarks. You deserve to lose. You deserve to lose EVERY WEEK.

Next week: Can BaseballPro77 recover from the psychological damage? Will the Wonderdogs keep wondering? And will someone please update the standings?

Stay thirsty, friends.
